paragraphs: I'm writing to apply for the Senior Full-Stack Engineer role. With over 7 years of cumulative technical experience (5+ years professional) building enterprise applications in C#/.NET, I've spent my career in domains — Insurance, Audit & Advisory, Supply Chain — that carry the same regulatory rigor and data-integrity demands as Financial Services and Asset Management, which aligns closely with what this role is looking for.

As Senior Developer at Delta Insurance, I drive end-to-end development across a .NET Framework legacy system and a .NET Core microservices platform with a React frontend and REST APIs, while managing cloud infrastructure on both Azure and AWS. Earlier, at Punongbayan & Araullo, I built internal ASP.NET/C#/Oracle SQL applications supporting audit and advisory operations, and used Azure DevOps for source control and CI/CD — direct experience with the enterprise-application, SQL, and Azure requirements this posting calls for.

I also actively use AI-assisted development tools — GitHub Copilot and Claude Code at Delta Insurance — to accelerate feature delivery and testing, which the posting specifically values. My Python experience is rooted in automation tooling (a Raspberry Pi/OpenCV telemetry system) rather than large-scale cloud-native services, and I don't have hands-on Java experience, but my depth across the rest of the .NET/Azure/SQL stack should more than offset that gap.
I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my background fits this role. Thank you for your consideration — I'm available to start on short notice given the contract timeline.
